LLM-as-judge — and the 4 ways it lies to you
How to build a golden set in one day

existing_cards.q: When should you NOT build a RAG?
existing_cards.a: When your knowledge base fits in the model's context window (Claude Sonnet/Opus 4.6+ ship a 1M-token window GA at standard pricing) — full-context + prompt caching is often faster and cheaper than building retrieval infra. Also skip RAG when answers don't depend on private or fresh data.

existing_cards.q: Sparse vs dense vs hybrid retrieval — when to use each?
existing_cards.a: Sparse (BM25) for rare terms, codes, names. Dense (vector) for paraphrase, semantic intent. Hybrid + reciprocal rank fusion is the production default. Add a cross-encoder re-ranker on top-k.

existing_cards.q: Why temperature=0 for extraction tasks?
existing_cards.a: Determinism + minimum hallucination. Pair with structured outputs and retry-on-invalid. Creative tasks want 0.5–0.8.

existing_cards.q: Three LLM eval layers?
existing_cards.a: Unit (deterministic checks: schema/regex/length/refusal), System (golden Q→A sets scored by LLM-as-judge + human spot-check), Production (online thumbs/escalation/regret).

existing_cards.q: Agent vs chain — what's the real distinction?
existing_cards.a: A chain has a fixed graph (LLM fills steps). An agent chooses the next step, including which tool to call. Agents trade determinism for flexibility — most "agents" should be chains.

existing_cards.q: Why does CoT (chain-of-thought) work?
existing_cards.a: Each token's prediction can attend to all prior tokens. Reasoning written FIRST means the answer benefits from that reasoning. Without CoT, the answer comes before any reasoning compute can be spent.

existing_cards.q: Production pattern for CoT + machine-readable output?
existing_cards.a: Use the provider's native structured outputs or tool-use schema (Anthropic extended thinking, OpenAI Structured Outputs) so reasoning lives in a separate thinking block and the final answer is schema-validated JSON. Reasoning is logged for debugging; only the validated JSON is returned to users.

existing_cards.q: Three reasons to fine-tune (vs RAG / prompting)?
existing_cards.a: (1) Narrow classification with latency budget. (2) Proprietary format / jargon / style the base model lacks. (3) Distillation: large→small for cost. NEVER for "teaching facts" — that's RAG.

existing_cards.q: Five controls for bounding agent autonomy?
existing_cards.a: (1) Action allowlist. (2) Per-conversation spend cap. (3) Max-step budget. (4) Human-in-the-loop on irreversible actions. (5) Per-step observability (every input/output logged).

existing_cards.q: Top failure mode in multi-agent systems?
existing_cards.a: Inter-agent coordination failures dominate (~37% of multi-agent bug reports per Cemri et al. 2025), and the biggest sub-pattern is the receiving agent missing context the sender assumed it had — wrong assumptions, dropped history, mismatched reasoning vs action. Fix: full trajectory logging, explicit state passing, and shared scratchpads instead of implicit handoffs.

existing_cards.q: Why is temperature 0 not perfectly deterministic in 2026?
existing_cards.a: Floating-point math is non-associative, and batched GPU inference reorders reductions depending on batch size and kernel scheduling — so identical inputs at T=0 can produce slightly different logits and occasionally different tokens. Treat T=0 as "very stable," not "deterministic"; always validate output and retry-on-invalid.

existing_cards.q: Best embedding choice for a customer requiring VPC-only deploys?
existing_cards.a: Self-hosted open-source models — BGE-M3 (Apache 2.0), Nomic Embed, GTE — run inside the VPC with no egress. API-based embeddings (OpenAI, Cohere, Voyage) need outbound network calls, so they're only an option if the customer accepts a private-deployment contract that runs the provider's model inside their VPC.

existing_cards.q: Anti-pattern in prompt injection defense?
existing_cards.a: Relying on the LLM's judgment to enforce policy. The LLM is generative — it can be fooled. Always add deterministic validators between LLM output and side effects (refunds, emails, deploys).

existing_cards.q: RAGAS metrics for RAG evaluation?
existing_cards.a: Faithfulness (answer uses retrieved context, not hallucinated), Answer relevance (addresses the question), Context precision / recall (retriever quality).

existing_cards.q: When should you use full-context loading over RAG?
existing_cards.a: When the knowledge base fits in the model's context window (Claude Sonnet/Opus 4.6+ support 1M tokens GA in 2026) and prompt caching is available — you get a simpler architecture, often lower cost via cache reads at ~10% of input price, and zero retrieval-miss risk.

existing_cards.q: Tagged-thinking pattern for CoT + structured output?
existing_cards.a: <thinking>...</thinking> then <answer>{json}</answer>. Parse only the answer; log thinking for debugging.

existing_cards.q: For a stable system prompt sent many times, what cuts cost ~90%?
existing_cards.a: Prompt caching. Provider caches the model's internal state for the prefix; subsequent requests reuse the computation.

existing_cards.q: Cross-encoder re-rank pipeline order?
existing_cards.a: Fast retrieval (top 50) → cross-encoder re-rank → top 5 → into LLM prompt. Cross-encoders too slow at full corpus scale.

existing_cards.q: Best chunk strategy for a structured contract with numbered clauses?
existing_cards.a: Structural splitting on clause hierarchy + recursive fallback for long clauses. Preserves meaning + makes citations precise.

existing_cards.q: Function calling vs MCP — what's the diff?
existing_cards.a: Function calling = each provider's native tool-use API (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) — tightly coupled to the model. MCP (Model Context Protocol) = open standard (donated to Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation in Dec 2025) for LLM↔tool integration; now supported by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft. Use MCP for portable tool servers, function calling for in-prompt one-offs.

existing_cards.q: Why do agent retries need idempotency keys?
existing_cards.a: Network blip → response lost → LLM retries → without idempotency keys, side effects (charges, emails) fire twice.

existing_cards.q: How do you detect LLM-judge drift?
existing_cards.a: Monthly: re-grade ~30 examples by hand, confirm judge agreement ≥ 85%. If drift, re-write rubric or swap judge model.

existing_cards.q: For a customer in a VPC, which embedding model?
existing_cards.a: Self-hostable open-source (BGE-M3 is the 2026 default; multi-lingual, dense+sparse) or domain-specific (e.g. voyage-law-2, voyage-code-3 — self-hostable for enterprise). Avoid API-only embedders (OpenAI text-embedding-3, Cohere) when egress from the VPC is prohibited.

existing_cards.q: Pinecone vs pgvector — when pgvector wins?
existing_cards.a: Customer already on Postgres + corpus under ~10M vectors (HNSW index handles this comfortably on a well-provisioned instance). Zero new infra; SQL filters compose with vector search. Migrate to Pinecone/Qdrant/etc. above ~50M or when you need horizontal autoscaling.

existing_cards.q: Three Anthropic-specific concepts to know?
existing_cards.a: (1) Constitutional AI. (2) Responsible Scaling Policy v3.0 (Feb 2026: two-track commitments + Frontier Safety Roadmap). (3) Long-context + prompt caching + extended thinking. Read the latest RSP and the Claude docs before interviewing.

existing_cards.q: Indirect prompt injection mitigation?
existing_cards.a: Tag retrieved/external content with <untrusted_content>...</untrusted_content> + instruct model to treat as DATA not instructions. Imperfect but reduces risk.

existing_cards.q: Speculative decoding explained?
existing_cards.a: Small fast model drafts tokens; big model validates in parallel. Most drafts pass → 2-3× speedup at big-model quality.

existing_cards.q: Voice agent UX hardest problem in 2026?
existing_cards.a: Turn-taking (detecting end-of-utterance via prosody + semantics, not VAD alone) and adaptive interruption handling (distinguishing backchannels like 'mm-hmm' from real barge-ins). End-to-end latency under ~600–700ms is necessary but not sufficient for 'feels human'.
